fv3_absolute_vorticity_cellmean.accuracy: exact addition of Coriolis to relative vorticity
fv3_absolute_vorticity_cellmean.reference: f = 2·Ω_rot·sin(lat); Harris et al. (2021), GFDL FV3 Technical Memorandum, §6.3; src/operators/vorticity.jl `fv_absolute_vorticity` (imperative reference).
fv3_absolute_vorticity_cellmean.notes: ω_abs = ω_rel + f, where f = 2·Omega_rot·sin(lat). Composes with `fv3_vorticity_cellmean`: the operand at the cell-center slot is the cell-mean relative vorticity emitted by that rule. The Coriolis offset is contributed by an additive constant term whose AST evaluates against the per-cell `lat` binding and the scalar `Omega_rot` binding (default 7.292e-5 rad/s for Earth). The two stencil rows are the cell-center relative vorticity (coeff 1) and the constant-source Coriolis term whose `coeff` AST is the closed-form 2·Omega_rot·sin(lat) — selector `kind: "constant"` flags a self-targeting source row that carries no operand read.

metric_bindings.Omega_rot: planetary rotation rate (rad/s); supplied as a scalar binding at stencil-application time
